title: tigridia purruchucana subsp. simplex
intro: Tigridia purruchucana subsp. simplex, also known as the Peruvian tiger flower or yellow tiger flower, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and tuberculosis. In terms of herbal preparations, Tigridia purruchucana subsp. simplex can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
